2026-04-07T07:07:35.0625773Z [36;1m# Mask immediately to prevent timing vulnerabilities[0m

If omitted, removes labels from the item that triggered this workflow.","type":"number"},"labels":{"description":"Label names to remove (e.g., ['bug', 'needs-triage']). Labels that don't exist on the item are silently skipped. At least one label must be provided.","items":{"type":"string"},"minItems":1,"type":"array"},"secrecy":{"description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\").","type":"string"}},"required":["labels"],"type":"object"},"name":"remove_labels"},{"description":"Assign one or more GitHub users to an issue. Use this to delegate work to specific team members. Users must have access to the repository.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"assignee":{"description":"Single GitHub username to assign. Use 'assignees' array for multiple users.","type":"string"},"assignees":{"description":"GitHub usernames to assign to the issue (e.g., ['octocat', 'mona']). Users must have access to the repository.","items":{"type":"string"},"type":"array"},"integrity":{"description":"Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., \"low\", \"medium\", \"high\").","type":"string"},"issue_number":{"description":"Issue number to assign users to. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 543 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/543). If omitted, assigns to the issue that triggered this workflow.","type":["number","string"]},"secrecy":{"description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\").","type":"string"}},"required":["issue_number"],"type":"object"},"name":"assign_to_user"},{"description":"Report that a tool or capability needed to complete the task is not available, or share any information you deem important about missing functionality or limitations. Use this when you cannot accomplish what was requested because the required functionality is missing or access is restricted.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"alternatives":{"description":"Any workarounds, manual steps, or alternative approaches the user could take (max 256 characters).","type":"string"},"integrity":{"description":"Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., \"low\", \"medium\", \"high\").","type":"string"},"reason":{"description":"Explanation of why this tool is needed or what information you want to share about the limitation (max 256 characters).","type":"string"},"secrecy":{"description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\").","type":"string"},"tool":{"description":"Optional: Name or description of the missing tool or capability (max 128 characters). Be specific about what functionality is needed.","type":"string"}},"required":["reason"],"type":"object"},"name":"missing_tool"},{"description":"Log a transparency message when no significant actions are needed. Use this to confirm workflow completion and provide visibility when analysis is complete but no changes or outputs are required (e.g., 'No issues found', 'All checks passed'). This ensures the workflow produces human-visible output even when no other actions are taken.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"integrity":{"description":"Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., \"low\", \"medium\", \"high\").","type":"string"},"message":{"description":"Status or completion message to log. Should explain what was analyzed and the outcome (e.g., 'Code review complete - no issues found', 'Analysis complete - all tests passing').","type":"string"},"secrecy":{"description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\").","type":"string"}},"required":["message"],"type":"object"},"name":"noop"},{"description":"Report that data or information needed to complete the task is not available. 2026-04-07T07:11:51.3415208Z {"timestamp":"2026-04-07T07:08:15.111311273Z","direction":"IN","type":"RESPONSE","server_id":"github","payload":{"id":1,"jsonrpc":"2.0","result":{"content":[{"text":"{\"number\":57855,\"title\":\"[ContainerServiceFleet] Docs: Add code examples and document new Gates and ManagedNamespace features\",\"body\":\"## Documentation Gap\\n\\n**Package:** `Azure.ResourceManager.ContainerServiceFleet`\\n**Service directory:** `sdk/fleet/Azure.ResourceManager.ContainerServiceFleet/`\\n**Triggered by:** PR #57844 by `@frantran`\\n\\n## What Changed\\n\\nPR #57844 generated version `1.2.0-beta.2` by upgrading the API version to `2025-08-01-preview`. This added two new resource types to the SDK:\\n\\n- **`ContainerServiceFleetGate` / `ContainerServiceFleetGateData` / `ContainerServiceFleetGateCollection`** ‚Äî Gates support for fleet-level upgrade coordination\\n- **`ContainerServiceFleetManagedNamespace` / `ContainerServiceFleetManagedNamespaceData` / `ContainerServiceFleetManagedNamespaceCollection`** ‚Äî Managed Namespace support\\n\\nCombined with features from `1.2.0-beta.1` (AutoUpgrade profiles, FleetMember labels, TargetKubernetesVersion), the package now has substantially more API surface than what the README describes.\\n\\n## Gaps Found\\n\\n- The README **Examples** section contains no inline code samples ‚Äî it only links to `(aka.ms/redacted), which is a generic redirect. There is no package-specific example showing how to create a Fleet, add a member, trigger an update run, create a Gate, or manage a ManagedNamespace.\\n- There are **no snippet placeholders** (`C# Snippet:`) in the README that would be populated by `Update-Snippets.ps1`.\\n- There is **no `tests/Samples/` directory** with hand-crafted sample test files. The only samples are the auto-generated ones under `tests/Generated/Samples/`, which are not wired into the README.\\n- The **Key concepts** section is a one-liner pointing to general SDK docs and says nothing specific to the Fleet service (e.g., what a Fleet is, what Members are, what Update Runs are, what Gates are, what Managed Namespaces are).\\n- The new **Gates** and **ManagedNamespace** features added in this and the prior beta are not mentioned or demonstrated anywhere in the README.\\n\\n\\n\\u003cstrong\\u003eüìê Implementation Guide\\u003c/strong\\u003e\\n\\nThis section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above.\\n\\n### Step 1: Update `README.md`\\n\\n**File:** `sdk/fleet/Azure.ResourceManager.ContainerServiceFleet/README.md`\\n**Action:** Edit\\n\\nReplace the placeholder **Key concepts** section with a concise description of the Fleet service domain model:\\n\\n```markdown\\n## Key concepts\\n\\n### Fleet\\n\\nA `Fleet` resource (represented by `ContainerServiceFleetResource`) is the top-level management object that groups AKS member clusters for coordinated lifecycle management.\\n\\n### Fleet Member\\n\\nA `ContainerServiceFleetMember` represents an AKS cluster enrolled in the fleet. Members can be labeled for targeted update strategies.\\n\\n### Update Run\\n\\nA `ContainerServiceFleetUpdateRun` orchestrates rolling upgrades across member clusters in defined stages and groups.\\n\\n### Update Strategy\\n\\nA `FleetUpdateStrategy` defines a reusable template of stages and groups for update runs.\\n\\n### Auto Upgrade Profile\\n\\nAn `AutoUpgradeProfile` automates Kubernetes version upgrades across fleet members based on release channel settings.\\n\\n### Gate\\n\\nA `ContainerServiceFleetGate` is a pre/post-upgrade checkpoint that can pause an update run until external conditions are satisfied, enabling integration with deployment pipelines.\\n\\n### Managed Namespace\\n\\nA `ContainerServiceFleetManagedNamespace` propagates a Kubernetes namespace (with RBAC, resource quotas, and network policies) across all fleet member clusters from the fleet hub.\\n```\\n\\nReplace the placeholder **Examples** section content with snippet placeholders (see Step 2):\\n\\n```markdown\\n## Examples\\n\\nThe following examples demonstrate common operations with the Azure Kubernetes Fleet Manager library.\\n\\n### Create a Fleet\\n\\n```\\n```\\n\\n### Add a Fleet Member\\n\\n```\\n```\\n\\n### Start an Update Run\\n\\n```\\n```\\n\\n### Create a Gate\\n\\n```\\n```\\n\\n### Create a Managed Namespace\\n\\n```\\n```\\n\\nFor more examples, see the [Azure SDK for .NET management library samples]((aka.ms/redacted)\\n```\\n\\n\\u0026gt; **Note:** The backtick fences above are abbreviated for clarity. In the actual README, use triple backticks on each opening and closing line as normal markdown code fences.\\n\\n### Step 2: Create sample test file\\n\\n**File:** `sdk/fleet/Azure.ResourceManager.ContainerServiceFleet/tests/Samples/Sample1_FleetOperations.cs`\\n**Action:** Create\\n\\n```csharp\\n// Copyright (c) Microsoft Corporation. This added two new resource types to the SDK:\\n\\n- **`ContainerServiceFleetGate` / `ContainerServiceFleetGateData` / `ContainerServiceFleetGateCollection`** ‚Äî Gates support for fleet-level upgrade coordination\\n- **`ContainerServiceFleetManagedNamespace` / `ContainerServiceFleetManagedNamespaceData` / `ContainerServiceFleetManagedNamespaceCollection`** ‚Äî Managed Namespace support\\n\\nCombined with features from `1.2.0-beta.1` (AutoUpgrade profiles, FleetMember labels, TargetKubernetesVersion), the package now has substantially more API surface than what the README describes.\\n\\n## Gaps Found\\n\\n- The README **Examples** section contains no inline code samples ‚Äî it only links to `(aka.ms/redacted), which is a generic redirect. There is no package-specific example showing how to create a Fleet, add a member, trigger an update run, create a Gate, or manage a ManagedNamespace.\\n- There are **no snippet placeholders** (`C# Snippet:`) in the README that would be populated by `Update-Snippets.ps1`.\\n- There is **no `tests/Samples/` directory** with hand-crafted sample test files. The only samples are the auto-generated ones under `tests/Generated/Samples/`, which are not wired into the README.\\n- The **Key concepts** section is a one-liner pointing to general SDK docs and says nothing specific to the Fleet service (e.g., what a Fleet is, what Members are, what Update Runs are, what Gates are, what Managed Namespaces are).\\n- The new **Gates** and **ManagedNamespace** features added in this and the prior beta are not mentioned or demonstrated anywhere in the README.\\n\\n\\n\\u003cstrong\\u003eüìê Implementation Guide\\u003c/strong\\u003e\\n\\nThis section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above.\\n\\n### Step 1: Update `README.md`\\n\\n**File:** `sdk/fleet/Azure.ResourceManager.ContainerServiceFleet/README.md`\\n**Action:** Edit\\n\\nReplace the placeholder **Key concepts** section with a concise description of the Fleet service domain model:\\n\\n```markdown\\n## Key concepts\\n\\n### Fleet\\n\\nA `Fleet` resource (represented by `ContainerServiceFleetResource`) is the top-level management object that groups AKS member clusters for coordinated lifecycle management.\\n\\n### Fleet Member\\n\\nA `ContainerServiceFleetMember` represents an AKS cluster enrolled in the fleet. Members can be labeled for targeted update strategies.\\n\\n### Update Run\\n\\nA `ContainerServiceFleetUpdateRun` orchestrates rolling upgrades across member clusters in defined stages and groups.\\n\\n### Update Strategy\\n\\nA `FleetUpdateStrategy` defines a reusable template of stages and groups for update runs.\\n\\n### Auto Upgrade Profile\\n\\nAn `AutoUpgradeProfile` automates Kubernetes version upgrades across fleet members based on release channel settings.\\n\\n### Gate\\n\\nA `ContainerServiceFleetGate` is a pre/post-upgrade checkpoint that can pause an update run until external conditions are satisfied, enabling integration with deployment pipelines.\\n\\n### Managed Namespace\\n\\nA `ContainerServiceFleetManagedNamespace` propagates a Kubernetes namespace (with RBAC, resource quotas, and network policies) across all fleet member clusters from the fleet hub.\\n```\\n\\nReplace the placeholder **Examples** section content with snippet placeholders (see Step 2):\\n\\n```markdown\\n## Examples\\n\\nThe following examples demonstrate common operations with the Azure Kubernetes Fleet Manager library.\\n\\n### Create a Fleet\\n\\n```\\n```\\n\\n### Add a Fleet Member\\n\\n```\\n```\\n\\n### Start an Update Run\\n\\n```\\n```\\n\\n### Create a Gate\\n\\n```\\n```\\n\\n### Create a Managed Namespace\\n\\n```\\n```\\n\\nFor more examples, see the [Azure SDK for .NET management library samples]((aka.ms/redacted)\\n```\\n\\n\\u0026gt; **Note:** The backtick fences above are abbreviated for clarity. In the actual README, use triple backticks on each opening and closing line as normal markdown code fences.\\n\\n### Step 2: Create sample test file\\n\\n**File:** `sdk/fleet/Azure.ResourceManager.ContainerServiceFleet/tests/Samples/Sample1_FleetOperations.cs`\\n**Action:** Create\\n\\n```csharp\\n// Copyright (c) Microsoft Corporation. ResourceIdentifier($\\u0026#34;/subscriptions/{subscriptionId}\\u0026#34;))\\n                .GetResourceGroupAsync(resourceGroupName);\\n\\n            ContainerServiceFleetCollection fleets = resourceGroup.GetContainerServiceFleets();\\n\\n            ContainerServiceFleetData fleetData = new ContainerServiceFleetData(AzureLocation.EastUS);\\n\\n            ArmOperation operation = await fleets.CreateOrUpdateAsync(\\n                WaitUntil.Completed,\\n                fleetName,\\n                fleetData);\\n\\n            ContainerServiceFleetResource fleet = operation.Value;\\n ContainerServiceFleetMemberData\\n            {\\n                ClusterResourceId = new ResourceIdentifier(aksClusterResourceId)\\n            };\\n\\n            ArmOperation operation = await members.CreateOrUpdateAsync(\\n                WaitUntil.Completed,\\n                \\u0026#34;\\u0026#34;,\\n                memberData);\\n\\n            ContainerServiceFleetMemberResource member = operation.Value;\\n            Console.WriteLine($\\u0026#34;Added member: {member.Data.Name}\\u0026#34;);\\n            #endregion\\n        }\\n\\n        [Test]\\n        [SyncOnly]\\n                \\u0026#34;\\u0026#34;,\\n                \\u0026#34;\\u0026#34;);\\n\\n            ContainerServiceFleetResource fleet = client.GetContainerServiceFleetResource(fleetId);\\n\\n
